title: thymus serpyllum var. ortmannianus
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. ortmannianus, also known as wild thyme, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, relieving respiratory problems, and boosting the immune system. The herb can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are often used to calm coughs, reduce stress, and promote relaxation. Additionally, wild thyme has been studied for its potential antiviral properties and its ability to support skin health, making it a popular ingredient in natural skincare products.
